anchor: Houseup — the proptech stack nobody is copying yet

posts.body_preview:
I spent 6 months inside Houseup's ops, rebuilding their property-matching engine.

The thing I learned: proptech founders keep trying to out-Zillow Zillow. The real moat is boring. It's the inbox.

Here's what we shipped 👇

The CRM template that powered a $40K → $1M ARR cleaning business.

8 slides. Every tab, every automation, every trigger. Free download in the comments.
Unpopular: 'AI-first' is a nothing phrase. Everything is AI-first now. The question is whether your agent can survive the third edge case your users throw at it.
I 2x'd a pest control company's revenue in 90 days with an SMS agent. Nobody wants to read about pest control. Everyone wants to read about AI. This is the mistake.
Three years running a services agency taught me one thing: the client wants the outcome, not the tool. If you lead with 'we use Claude / n8n / Retell' you've already lost the room.
Proptech's real moat isn't the map view. It's the inbox. 8 slides on what we shipped for Houseup — swipe to see the agent handling a 3 AM rental enquiry end-to-end.
Every proptech founder thinks they need to out-Zillow Zillow. They don't. They need an agent that replies in 30 seconds at 3 AM. 90-second rant with the data.
Back-of-house story: prepping 60 covers tonight. Agent handled every catering intake email today — I haven't opened Gmail since 9 AM.
